message:
feat: add credential ID injection support and secretless auth to
Semantic Scholar connector

Enable the Semantic Scholar connector to authenticate via egress proxy
credential injection (X-FCP-Credential-Id header) as an alternative
to direct API key embedding, improving security posture.

connectors/semanticscholar/src/client.rs:
- Extend SemanticScholarAuth enum with CredentialId(CredentialId)
  variant for proxy-based credential injection
- New is_secretless() method: returns true for credential ID auth
  (no secret material held in-process)
- Implement redaction logic: credential IDs show truncated prefix in
  logs, API keys are fully masked
- Request building now inserts X-FCP-Credential-Id header when using
  credential ID auth, instead of x-api-key

connectors/semanticscholar/src/connector.rs:
- Parameter parsing: accept either api_key or credential_id (mutually
  exclusive) with validation error on conflict
- CredentialId type parsing with format validation
- Self-check report now distinguishes auth modes (api_key vs
  credential_id vs public) in provisioning readiness output

connectors/semanticscholar/tests/integration.rs:
- New setup_connector_credential_id() fixture for credential-based
  test scenarios
- New tests: configure with credential_id, doctor self-check with
  credential auth, verify correct auth mode reporting in self-check
  response

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
